title: cohniella pongratziana
intro: Cohniella pongratziana, a species of fungus in the family Laboulbeniaceae, is a type of insect-associated fungus that grows on certain insect hosts, particularly beetles. The medicinal properties of Cohniella pongratziana include its potential antibacterial and antifungal activities, which have been studied in scientific research. The health benefits of this fungus are still being researched, but it has been suggested that it may have immunomodulatory effects and potentially aid in the treatment of certain infections. Cohniella pongratziana is typically studied in a laboratory setting, and herbal preparations are not commonly available, but extracts from the fungus have been used in scientific studies to investigate its medicinal properties.
